question: What is the verified current status as of February 23, 2026 of the CLARITY Act and GENIUS Act according to Congress.gov and official Treasury Department guidance—specifically: enactment date, implementation timelines, effective dates for each major provision, and any delays or amendments since January 2026?

research.summary: As of February 23, 2026, the GENIUS Act is enacted law (signed July 18, 2025) with implementation in multi-year phases culminating in a July 18, 2026 regulatory deadline for Treasury rulemaking. The CLARITY Act remains pending and has not been enacted—it was delayed in committee on January 14, 2026, with no new markup date announced, though sources project possible passage by April 2026.

findings.claim: GENIUS Act was signed into law on July 18, 2025
findings.reasoning: Multiple authoritative sources including Paul Hastings, Lw.com, Morgan Lewis, and JDSupra all confirm the July 18, 2025 enactment date with direct presidential signing by President Trump

findings.claim: GENIUS Act requires 1:1 reserve backing for payment stablecoins and federal licensing for payment stablecoin issuers (PPSIs)
findings.reasoning: Congressional Research Service (CRS) report and Sullivan & Cromwell document directly describe core requirements

findings.claim: GENIUS Act implementation has a July 18, 2026 regulatory deadline for Treasury rulemaking (1 year from enactment)
findings.reasoning: Multiple sources confirm 18-month implementation period and July 18, 2026 deadline; Congress.gov text specifies Treasury must complete arrangements 'not later than 2 years after enactment'; Federal Register advance notice of proposed rulemaking issued September 19, 2025

findings.claim: GENIUS Act full implementation by January 2027 at latest, with provisions taking effect in multi-year phases
findings.reasoning: Paul Hastings and other legal sources confirm multi-year implementation period; no immediate effect for most provisions; staggered timeline for rulemaking and compliance

findings.claim: CLARITY Act (H.R. 3633) is still pending—not enacted as of February 23, 2026
findings.reasoning: Congress.gov shows status as 'Introduced in House (05/29/2025)'; January 14, 2026 committee markup delayed with no new date; multiple sources confirm still pending in early 2026

findings.claim: CLARITY Act committee markup was delayed on January 14, 2026 with no new date announced
findings.reasoning: Baker McKenzie article dated January 14, 2026 reports delay by committee leadership; Yahoo Finance confirms subsequent Senate delays

findings.claim: CLARITY Act projected to potentially clear Congress by April 2026
findings.reasoning: Blocklr projection based on current momentum, but this is forward-looking estimate not confirmed legislative schedule; projections are inherently uncertain

findings.claim: CLARITY Act provisions on stablecoin yields must be reconciled with GENIUS Act framework due by July 18, 2026
findings.reasoning: Blocklr notes coordination requirement between two acts' provisions; however, CLARITY Act not yet enacted so reconciliation timeline is prospective

knowledge_gaps: Specific CLARITY Act provisions currently in committee review as of February 2026 (sections under negotiation, controversial language)
Detailed breakdown of GENIUS Act phase-in schedule—which provisions take effect at what dates between July 2026 and January 2027
Treasury Department guidance documents issued between September 2025 (ANPRM) and February 2026—status of proposed rulemaking phase
Any amendments or modifications to GENIUS Act between January-February 2026 (sources do not indicate amendments, but comprehensive Treasury guidance not located)
Specific House-Senate negotiations on CLARITY Act compromise language as of mid-February 2026
OCC conditional charter implementation status relative to GENIUS Act framework as of February 2026

strengths: Excellent specificity with precise dates, confidence levels, and source attribution
Strong intellectual honesty - clearly distinguishes established facts from projections and flags uncertainties
High accuracy in factual claims with appropriate sourcing from authoritative legal and government sources
Good knowledge gap identification showing awareness of research limitations
Clear distinction between enacted (GENIUS) and pending (CLARITY) legislation status
weaknesses: Limited depth in explaining the mechanisms and implications of the legislation beyond basic requirements
Completeness gaps in implementation details and regulatory process status
Minimal analysis of the substantive differences between GENIUS and CLARITY Acts beyond timing
Lacks exploration of industry impact, stakeholder responses, or practical implementation challenges
critique.actionable_feedback: To improve this research: 1) Expand depth by explaining the regulatory mechanisms (how 1:1 backing works, what federal licensing entails, enforcement structure), 2) Include more implementation details by searching Treasury guidance documents and rulemaking progress, 3) Add analysis of substantive policy differences between the two acts, 4) Research industry and stakeholder responses to understand practical implications, 5) Investigate any enforcement actions or early compliance activities under GENIUS Act
